raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/XXXX my computer was compromised from someone posing as someone from Best Buy- XXXX XXXX XXXX Everything seemed to be legitimate. I wanted to cancel my subscription and they agreed to take care of that by asking me to chat online with them which I did. When they asked for my XXXX, I knew then something was not right. I immediately got out of the screen. My husband and I went to our Best Buy store and they confirmed that I was not the first person to be scammed with this " XXXX XXXX '' email. 
I immediately contacted my credit card bank, Citi Bank, and they said that a charge of {$9.00} had been made to my account and then immediately afterward a charge of {$140.00} was made. They said that those charges would be disputed and I would be reimbursed and not responsible for those charges. Then on XX/XX/XXXX, I received an email from Citi Bank stating that the reimbursement would be reversed that they had information that this was a legitimate charge. 
Now I am furious because I did not make or agree to this transaction. I started calling the bank, getting tossed from person to person in their " Security Dept '' who makes all kinds of excuses as to why this has ocurred and they will look into it. 
It is now XX/XX/XXXX, I have spoken with XXXX, XXXX, XXXX, XXXX, XXXX, XXXX, XXXX, and XXXX, all from the Security Dept at Citi card Bank. I call every week and get the same runaround. This so- called Security team is a joke and do nothing to help their customers. I am told the same story over and over ... .they will look into it. 
I realize that this is not a huge amount of money. But the fact remains, I did not receive any product, I did not order anything, and I did not give anyone authorization to use my card! This was done by someone who is scamming people and obviously getting away with it. I just want my account credited with the {$140.00} that is due to me.
